target.name: review-report
target.source_summary: templates/review-report.md + agents/architect.md (Review Checklist, Must Not) + agents/tester.md (Core Behavior) + Delivery Constitution review defaults

gates.description: The verdict matches the findings: any High-severity blocker with verdict 'approved' (or 'approved with conditions' where a condition is itself a blocker) fails. A verdict either follows from the findings or it doesn't.
Findings touching security, billing, auth, state integrity, or deployment are never waved through as conditions or cosmetic items — Review Defaults fail closed on these.

dimensions.description: Findings are located, evidenced, and explained.

anchors.1: Findings are impressions: 'the code could be cleaner', 'error handling looks weak' — no location, no evidence.
anchors.3: Located to a file/surface, but evidence is assertion rather than a quoted span, traced flow, or named pattern violation.
anchors.5: Every finding has location + quoted/cited evidence + why-it-matters, per the template's finding shape.

dimensions.description: Each finding carries a remediation the owning role can execute.

anchors.1: Findings without required remediation, or remediation restates the problem ('fix the auth check').
anchors.3: Remediation names the change but not where it lives or how to verify it.
anchors.5: Each remediation names the change, its location, and the verification that would close it — directly executable as a bounce instruction.

dimensions.description: Blockers and cosmetic issues are cleanly distinguished and correctly tiered.

anchors.1: Cosmetic and blocking findings interleaved without severity, or 'collapsed into vague summaries'.
anchors.3: Severity assigned, but at least one finding is mis-tiered (style nit marked High, broken wiring marked Low).
anchors.5: Severity ordering matches real risk; cosmetic items present but explicitly non-blocking.

dimensions.description: The review works the applicable checklist (architect's 8 checks, or the audit scope) rather than sampling.

anchors.1: Three or fewer checklist areas addressed, the rest silent.
anchors.3: Most areas addressed; gaps exist and are not acknowledged.
anchors.5: Every applicable area addressed or explicitly marked not-applicable with reason.

dimensions.description: Non-blocking risks remain visible with their escalation conditions.

anchors.1: Residual risks section empty although conditions/findings imply open risk.
anchors.5: Each residual risk named with the condition under which it would become a blocker.

aggregation.gate_handling: Apply gate caps after normalization; lowest applicable cap wins.

weight_rationale: finding_specificity is heaviest because the philosophy names review as 'a real function, not a ceremonial step' — unevidenced findings are exactly what makes review ceremonial, and every downstream remediation inherits their vagueness.

unmeasurable_rules.rule: The reviewer actually read what it critiques
Do not approve dependency-blind plans (plan-review case)
unmeasurable_rules.home: Partially in checklist_coverage; dependency blindness itself is graded by task-plan.rubric.json on the reviewed artifact.

known_good.summary: Blocked review with located, remediable findings.
known_good.content: Verdict: blocked. Findings — High: 'workers/export.js:18 re-verifies the session (verifyAuth call) inside the handler; trust boundary is verify once in the Worker auth guard. Why: re-verification hell makes the authoritative check ambiguous. Remediation: delete handler check, consume requestContext.user; verify by request trace showing single auth check.' High: 'src/utils/export-job.js claims work without atomic claim (read-then-update); two consumers can double-process. Remediation: claim via UPDATE ... WHERE status=pending RETURNING; verify with concurrent harness run.' Low (cosmetic, non-blocking): inconsistent log prefixes. Residual: export size unbounded — becomes a blocker if R2 object >100MB appears. Next step: engineer fixes both Highs; re-review wiring only.

known_bad.summary: Approves despite naming an auth hole.
known_bad.content: Verdict: approved with conditions. Findings — Medium: 'auth check in the export handler seems off, worth a look sometime.' Low: 'code style varies.' Conditions: 'tidy up before next sprint.' Residual risks: none. Next step: ship it.
